channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'I think EmDash was created to sell more Cloudflare services': WordPress co-founder Matt Mullenweg gives his verdict on Cloudflare's EmDash
item.description: WordPress co-founder Mullenweg isn't too critical about EmDash or Cloudflare, says he sees it as a commercial opportunity, not a 'spiritual successor'.

item.title: The Best iPad to Buy (and Some to Avoid) in 2026: Compare the Air, Pro, Mini
item.description: We break down the current iPad lineup to help you figure out which of Apple’s tablets is best for you.

item.title: Attention, les virements bancaires ne fonctionnent pas ce week-end : voici deux alternatives
item.description: Pendant tout le week-end, du vendredi 3 avril au lundi 6 avril 2026, les systèmes européens de paiement interbancaires sont à l'arrêt. Les virements ne fonctionnent plus. Il existe heureusement des alternatives.

item.title: L’autonomie est le cadet des soucis du Oppo A6x en promotion, qui propose en plus de la 5G à très bon prix
item.description: D'abord lancé en Inde fin 2025, l'Oppo A6x 5G débarque en France à un prix attractif de 199,90 euros, en baisse à 179,90 euros sur le site de la marque, un téléphone à l'autonomie XL.

item.title: The Morning After: NASA’s Artemis II is on a voyage around the Moon
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II successfully launched on April 1, with its crew on a 10-day mission to circle the Moon. It’s the first crewed Artemis flight and a major step toward humanity returning to our little neighbor in the future. Since launch, the vehicle has separated from its launch system and been manually piloted, testing how the Orion capsule will dock with future lunar landers. There have been some snags, however: The onboard toilet went awry, and Microsoft Outlook has been acting screwy. Jokes aside, there is something magnificent about seeing humanity taking to the stars once again. That, fo...

item.title: A Year After DOGE Cuts, GSA Now Plans to Hire Hundreds of Employees
item.description: The General Services Administration is hiring “approximately 400 positions,” according to an internal email viewed by WIRED. Last year the agency lost thousands of workers as part of DOGE’s rampage.

item.title: The leadership dilemma: Governing the “Agentic AI” workforce
item.description: Agentic AI is reshaping the workforce, however, without strong governance, autonomy can become organizational risk.

item.title: Oscar Isaac Did ‘X-Men: Apocalypse’ to Work With Top Tier Actors—Instead, He Got a Cooling Tent
item.description: The 'Star Wars' star is once again looking back on his 'X-Men' experience, and finding it wanting. And sweaty.

item.title: New Garmin Training Features (2026): Nutrition Tracking, Lifestyle Logging, and More
item.description: Nutrition tracking? Gear tracking? Garmin’s latest software updates aim to add value to the company’s line of premium sports watches.

item.title: Best Game Controllers for PC, Switch, PS5, and Xbox
item.description: A great gamepad instantly levels up your play. These are our top picks for Switch, Xbox, PlayStation, and PC.

item.title: Le Oppo Find X9 Ultra détrône-t-il le Galaxy S26 Ultra ? On a comparé les premiers résultats
item.description: Officiellement présenté le 21 avril, le Oppo Find X9 Ultra apparaît sur Geekbench. On a comparé ses performances avec ses concurrents.

item.title: NASA's Artemis II mission has left Earth's orbit
item.description: The Integrity, the name of the Orion capsule carrying Artemis II astronauts on a trip around the moon, has left Earth’s orbit. NASA has announced that it has successfully completed a key burn of Orion’s main engine. That six-minute firing of the engine provided approximately 6,000 pounds of thrust, which gave the spacecraft the acceleration it needed to set it on its path to the moon. “Today, for the first time since Apollo 17 in 1972, humans have departed Earth orbit,” said Dr. Lori Glaze from NASA Headquarters in Washington. “Orion is operating with crew for the first time in space, and we a...

item.title: The 3 Best Portable Jump Starters in 2026: Get Charged Up
item.description: The new crop of portable jump starters means you'll never be stuck waiting on a tow. Here are the devices we'd trust.

item.title: How CIOs can create a strong foundation for an AI-enabled workplace
item.description: CIOs must pair AI tools with capability, governance and leadership to raise workplace standards.

item.title: Trump’s Economy Has Come for Sugar Babies
item.description: Amid the latest economic downturn, sugar babies are asking their daddies for stock market advice and taking on vanilla jobs to recession-proof their incomes.

item.title: Artemis II will use laser beams in giant leap for space video — new optical tech will stream '4K high-definition video from the Moon'
item.description: As the Artemis II mission continues towards the moon, we should be treated to some high-def video soon.

item.title: Watch out Microsoft 365 — European giants launch Euro-Office, a 'true sovereign office suite'
item.description: Microsoft has already been hit by monopoly investigations, but now Europe is planning to launch its own Office rival.

item.title: L’excellente Withings ScanWatch 2 surveille votre santé pour moins de 300 € grâce à ce pack chez Boulanger
item.description: La Withings ScanWatch 2 est une montre connectée élégante qui cumule les fonctions et les mesures de santé. Actuellement, Boulanger la propose dans un pack à 299,95 euros au lieu de 349,95 euros, et un bracelet est même offert.

item.title: iOS 26.4 : Apple intègre enfin ChatGPT à CarPlay, mais avec un gros défaut
item.description: L'intelligence artificielle s'invite enfin au volant, mais Apple et Google empruntent des routes radicalement différentes. La mise à jour iOS 26.4 intègre un ChatGPT sous haute surveillance à CarPlay. Alors que dans le même temps, Google lâche les chevaux en imposant son modèle ultra-bavard Gemini sur Android Auto.

item.title: Alogic Edge 40-inch 5K ultrawide monitor review: This stunning Mac-friendly display is outstanding for creatives — but calibration is needed before use
item.description: Keep your workspace clear with this huge 21:9 40-inch 5K monitor. It’s an excellent pick for studios and creative offices.

item.title: Google Cast débarque sur les TV Samsung : fini le casse-tête pour diffuser des contenus sans fil
item.description: Samsung déploie actuellement une mise à jour que l’on attendait depuis longtemps sur ses téléviseurs connectés pour y intégrer nativement Google Cast. Une excellente surprise pour les modèles déjà installés dans vos salons, qui n'auront plus besoin du moindre dongle externe pour communiquer avec vos téléphones.

item.title: Artemis II : reflex d’époque, hybride de dernière minute et smartphones… l’étrange arsenal photo de la Nasa
item.description: Pour immortaliser le premier vol habité vers la Lune depuis un demi-siècle, la Nasa a décidé de mélanger des technologies très différentes. Si l'agence spatiale reste fidèle à de robustes reflex sortis en 2016 pour les clichés officiels, elle a accepté d'embarquer un appareil hybride dernier cri à la demande insistante de l'équipage. Une petite révolution technologique couplée à l'autorisation inédite des smartphones personnels pour documenter cette aventure sous un angle intimiste....

item.title: 'Everything is becoming AI intelligent and AI-enabled': Oracle database head says agents are the future, but warns “there's no magic bullet”
item.description: The AI agent-powered workforce is coming sooner than you’d expect, Oracle database head tells us.

item.title: Artemis II en route pour la Lune : une bascule cruciale, puis une vue au hublot inoubliable
item.description: Après avoir quitté l’orbite terrestre grâce à l’injection translunaire, l’équipage d’Artemis II profite de son voyage vers la Lune pour se familiariser avec Orion, partager ses premières impressions et préparer des observations scientifiques uniques. Entre moments d’émerveillement face à la...

item.title: Samsung Frame Pro and OLED TV News: What You Need To Know in 2026
item.description: Samsung leans into its artistic side with its newest TVs.

item.title: 'An exceptional audio investment': Audio-Technica's new open-backs just jumped straight to the top of my headphone pile (and it's not even close)
item.description: Audio-Technica's flagship open-back headphones are deliciously detailed and pack a serious low-end punch, but they're very expensive

item.title: The Chromecast may be dead, but Samsung is rolling out Google Cast support to its TV line-ups for free
item.description: Samsung is pushing out One UI Tizen updates that expand Google Cast functionality to older TV models.

item.title: AI has come for Domino’s pizza tracker, and we’re not mad about it
item.description: Domino’s pizza tracker gets a new look and AI brains that will pinpoint your pizza’s delivery time. While companies cram artificial intelligence features you never asked for into their apps, Domino’s seems to have found a valid use case for the technology: more accurate tracking of when your pizza will be ready.

item.title: California just hit an inflection point for batteries
item.description: California’s electric grid just broke a new record, providing as much power during peak demand as six Hoover Dams. A few days ago, the electric grid in California hit a new milestone: At 7pm on March 29, batteries provided 12.3 gigawatts of power—roughly as much as six Hoover Dams, or around 43% of the total demand on the grid.

item.title: Many productivity programs solve the wrong problem. This is what leaders should do instead
item.description: You can’t design a system that works for everyone. Most organizations think they have a productivity problem. They don’t. They have a work design problem.

item.title: The architecture world just got its second union
item.description: Sage & Coombe Architects unanimously approved a collective bargaining agreement, making it the second firm in the U.S. to ratify a contract. This week, the labor movement in architecture scored a win. Sage & Coombe Architects, a women-led firm based in New York City, unanimously approved a collective bargaining agreement. It’s the second American practice to ratify a contract, after Bernheimer Architecture in 2024....

item.title: The cosmetic surgery industry is mainly built for women. So why is it run by men?
item.description: Representation at the top isn’t just a diversity issue. It’s a competitive advantage. In female-driven industries, leadership misalignment shows up in the numbers long before it shows up in headlines.

item.title: Le patron de Xiaomi démonte la nouvelle SU7 électrique en direct sur Internet
item.description: Le patron de Xiaomi, Lei Jun, a procédé au démontage intégral d’une SU7 de nouvelle génération en direct sur Internet. L’homme d’affaires donne de nombreuses informations sur la voiture électrique, qui évolue sur de nombreux points.

item.title: Proton VPN à -70 % : l’un des meilleurs VPN au monde brade son abonnement premium [Sponso]
item.description: Profiter de la protection avancée d’un VPN suisse est désormais à la portée de tous. La preuve avec cette offre exceptionnelle de Proton VPN à seulement 2,99 euros par mois.

item.title: Windows 11 : Microsoft commence à forcer la mise à niveau vers 25H2 mais c’est pour votre bien
item.description: La fin du support de Windows 11 24H2 approchant, Microsoft vient de commencer à forcer la mise à niveau de machines fonctionnant toujours sur cette version de Windows 11 vers Windows 11 25H2, la version la plus récente du système d’exploitation.
